decision_summary: AC-020 did not close in Pack 5. Mutex track and parse-cost track both ruled out via clean falsification (B.1+C.1+E.1 all REVERT with explicit evidence). Residual concurrent contention is architectural (rusqlite-side or ReaderPool Rust-side Mutex, or WAL shared-memory atomics) — out of Pack 5 scope without a separate ADR. Hand off to a follow-up packet with D.1-class architectural intervention as the starting point.

outstanding_open_questions.question: Does sqlite3_config actually take effect when invoked from register_sqlite_vec_extension?
outstanding_open_questions.answer: YES at the register_sqlite_vec_extension callsite it returned SQLITE_MISUSE = 21 silently (per §5 entry); B.1 #2 proved that at Engine::open_locked head it returns SQLITE_OK = 0. Capturing the rc is what differentiates the two attempts.

outstanding_open_questions.question: Is the 1.5/AC020_THREADS bound the right contract, or should AC-020 specify the contention source?
outstanding_open_questions.note: Pack 5 evidence supports a contract refinement: the bound-as-ratio is correct only when the contention source is the SQLite threading layer. Now that we know the residual is upstream of SQLite (rusqlite/ReaderPool Mutex) or sideways (WAL atomics), the next packet may want to specify the bound against a named runtime invariant rather than a black-box ratio.

outstanding_open_questions.question: For deployments using host-system SQLite (non-bundled), SQLITE_THREADSAFE is fixed at the host-distro build flag. Document this.
outstanding_open_questions.answer: Now moot: C.1 demonstrated that even with bundled-only THREADSAFE=2 the AC-020 ratio doesn't move. Recommendation: stay bundled-default at THREADSAFE=1; do not require bundled-only deployment for AC-020 reasons.

data_for_pivot: Next packet's hypothesis: the contended primitive is the rusqlite-side Mutex wrapping the sqlite3 handle, OR our own ReaderPool's Mutex<Vec<Connection>> at lib.rs:154, OR WAL shared-memory atomics. First experiment should be a perf re-capture against the current 0.6.0-rewrite tip (post-revert state, cleaner symbols available) classifying which Mutex domain the spinlock cycles surface from. If rusqlite Mutex: the fix is either (a) replace rusqlite with raw sqlite3-sys + manual handle management (high LoC, high risk), or (b) increase READER_POOL_SIZE to spread contention. If ReaderPool Mutex: replace Mutex<Vec<Connection>> with a lock-free pool (crossbeam ArrayQueue or SegQueue). If WAL atomics: switch to WAL2 (SQLite 3.45+ feature; not yet in libsqlite3-sys-0.30) or reduce WAL pressure (separate readers from writers physically). The lock-free pool replacement is the smallest-radius change with the highest-evidence support and is recommended as the next-packet starting point.
next_packet_pointer: Phase 9 Pack 6 — architectural reader-pool refactor. Hypothesis: replace ReaderPool's Mutex<Vec<Connection>> with crossbeam::queue::ArrayQueue for lock-free borrow/release, then re-measure AC-020. Expected impact based on Pack 5 evidence: removes ~6.8% concurrent cycles attributed to ___pthread_mutex_lock + ~1.8% lll_mutex_lock_optimized + reductions in __aarch64_swp4_rel/__aarch64_cas4_acq from CAS contention on the pool slot. Combined with perf re-capture to confirm the source, expected concurrent drop 30-50% if the verdict is correct.
unexpected_observations: Two important meta-findings from Pack 5: (1) The orchestrator-subagent chaining bug (B.1 attempt #1) was caught early but cost a full Opus-high spawn cycle; the resume §4 anti-chaining defenses (PREAMBLE + --disallowedTools Task Agent + stream-json) worked first try on every spawn after fc3dda3. Apply to all future packets. (2) The B.1 prompt's sqlite3_threadsafe()==2 assertion was a real spec error inherited from whitepaper §7.3 — corrected here in §7.3 + §11 + the prompt itself. Future spec writers should validate runtime-observable assertions against vendor docs before locking them into a decision rule.
agent_verify_status: deferred (final synthesis is doc-only; no source change to verify since the last green run inside C.1's worktree at 15c6473)
escalation_notes: Per resume §9 escalation triggers: 'A-phase produces no clear single-bottleneck signal' is partially true — A.2 produced a clear mutex_atomic signal, but B.1+C.1 falsified it as the *primary* driver, leaving the architectural residual as the unresolved next-step. AC-018 stayed green throughout, no data-loss risk discovered, no Phase 7/8 invariant break (codex Phase 7/8 cross-check PASS on the only diff that landed = E.1, then reverted). Recommended human action: approve Pack 6 (architectural reader-pool refactor) per next_packet_pointer above; or explicitly defer AC-020 and ship 0.6.0 with the gate as a known-not-met item documented in test-plan.md.
